findings.mitigation: 1. Configure a site-wide Content-Security-Policy that includes object-src 'none' to block all plugin/object/embed usage. 2. Apply the CSP header to all responses, including the /vulnerabilities/csp/ path. 3. Enable CSP violation reporting (report-uri or report-to) to capture violations during rollout. 4. Verify the header is present and object-src is restricted using curl -I https://pentest-ground.com:4280/vulnerabilities/csp/ and by checking browser console for CSP violations.
findings.remediation: 1. Implement a centralized CSP in the codebase or framework middleware with object-src 'none' (or restricted to trusted domains). 2. If embedding is required, whitelist only trusted domains in object-src (e.g., object-src 'self' https://trusted.example.com). 3. Add CSP violation reporting (report-to or report-uri) and monitor results; adjust policy as needed. 4. Re-test across all endpoints; ensure the vulnerable path /vulnerabilities/csp/ shows the policy in effect and no CSP warnings.
findings.llm_notes: CSP and cookie flags misconfig on the web app expose multiple attack surfaces.
findings.exploitability: Attacker could exploit if they can inject or control content on the page (e.g., via XSS) to embed resources from attacker-controlled domains. Missing object-src CSP means <object>/<embed> content isn't restricted, enabling potential drive-by content delivery in a trust context.

findings.mitigation:
1. Nginx: add a CSP header with base-uri 'self' to all responses.
```nginx
# In the server block
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'";
```
2. Apache: apply the same CSP header at vhost or .htaccess level.
```apache
# In vhost or .htaccess
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'"
```
3. PHP: ensure CSP header is emitted from application bootstrap for dynamic pages.
```php
header("Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'");
```
4. Validation: verify header presence on vulnerable path and across pages.
```bash
curl -sI https://pentest-ground.com:4280/vulnerabilities/csp/ | grep -i Content-Security-Policy
```
findings.llm_notes: CSP not configured; HttpOnly and Secure flags are missing on cookies; multiple header misconfigurations indicate broad security posture weaknesses (XSS/resource loading risk).
findings.exploitability: Prereq: victim visits a page on the domain. Attacker can deliver inline or external script payloads since CSP is not enforced; this enables script execution and potential cookie/session data exfiltration. No special privileges required.

findings.mitigation: 1. Configure the web server to send clickjacking protections on all responses: X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN. 2. Add a Content-Security-Policy header that includes frame-ancestors to prevent framing: Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'. 3. Ensure any reverse proxy/CDN or application layer preserves these headers and does not strip or override them. 4. Validate by re-scanning and verifying headers in responses (e.g., curl -I https://pentest-ground.com:4280 | grep -i -E 'frame-ancestors|x-frame-options').
findings.remediation: 1. Implement a site-wide CSP policy that includes frame-ancestors 'self' and other prudent directives: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; frame-ancestors 'self'. 2. Apply the policy at the web server or application framework level so it covers all endpoints and cannot be bypassed. 3. Retain X-Frame-Options as a fallback header for older clients. 4. Re-test with automated scanners and manual checks to confirm the CSP and X-Frame-Options headers are present on all responses and that the site cannot be embedded in iframes from external origins.
findings.llm_notes: Security headers misconfiguration detected: CSP not set, frame options and other headers missing; remediate CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, and cookie security flags.
findings.exploitability: No CSP frame-ancestors and missing X-Frame-Options enable clickjacking via an attacker-controlled page embedding the site; no authentication required, user visits the attacker page.

findings.title: TLS/SSL misconfigurations
findings.description:
OCSP Must-Staple extension is missing
Path: /

raw.http_request:
GET / HTTP/1.1
host: pentest-ground.com:4280
connection: keep-alive
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:128.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/128.0
accept-language: en-US
accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
raw._poc_evidence:
PoC poc-009 (python) — verdict: inconclusive
Exit code: 0
--- stdout ---
Trying CBC cipher: ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-SHA256
Handshake might have failed or no cipher negotiated for this cipher.
Trying CBC cipher: ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-SHA384
Handshake might have failed or no cipher negotiated for this cipher.
CBC_Cipher_Negotiated: NO

--- stderr ---

findings.mitigation:
1. Enable OCSP stapling on the TLS terminator to deliver OCSP responses to clients. Verify after enabling.

Nginx example:
```nginx
ssl_stapling on;
ssl_stapling_verify on;
resolver 8.8.8.8 8.8.4.4;
ssl_trusted_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt;
```

Apache example:
```apache
SSLUseStapling on
SSLStaplingCache shmcb:/var/run/ssl_stapling(256k)
```

2. Disable CBC ciphers and enforce AEAD ciphers; enable TLSv1.2+ and TLSv1.3 where available.

Nginx:
```nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```

Apache:
```apache
SSLProtocol All -SSLv2 -SSLv3 -TLSv1 -TLSv1.1
SSLCipherSuite ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
SSLHonorCipherOrder On
```

3. If possible, request a certificate with OCSP Must-Staple (TLS Feature: status_request) and redeploy.

4. Validate with a quick check:
```bash
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:4280 -servername pentest-ground.com -tls1_2 -status
```
findings.remediation:
1. Reissue certificate with OCSP Must-Staple (TLS Feature: status_request) and install the new chain on all hosts.

2. Enable and verify OCSP stapling across all TLS endpoints (Nginx/Apache) using the same configs as mitigation, and confirm stapling is active.

3. Harden TLS configuration across all environments: disable legacy protocols (SSLv2/SSLv3/TLSv1/TLSv1.1), enable TLSv1.2+ and TLSv1.3, and use AEAD ciphers.

Nginx:
```nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```

Apache:
```apache
SSLProtocol All -SSLv2 -SSLv3 -TLSv1 -TLSv1.1
SSLCipherSuite ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
SSLHonorCipherOrder On
```

4. Validate deployment and monitor: run TLS/OCSP tests and set alerts for stapling failures or certificate issues.

Example test:
```bash
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:4280 -servername pentest-ground.com -status
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS misconfiguration: OCSP Must-Staple is not enforced; address by enabling OCSP stapling and improving TLS headers (HSTS, CSP, Secure/HttpOnly cookies).
findings.exploitability: Requires network access to the target as an on-path attacker; without OCSP stapling, a compromised OCSP responder could mislead clients about certificate status, enabling a MITM under some conditions.

--- stdout ---
Trying CBC cipher: ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-SHA256
Handshake might have failed or no cipher negotiated for this cipher.
Trying CBC cipher: ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-SHA384
Handshake might have failed or no cipher negotiated for this cipher.

--- stderr ---

findings.mitigation:
1. Disable insecure TLS renegotiation across all components and ensure secure renegotiation is negotiated. 2. Upgrade TLS stack to versions with RFC 5746 secure renegotiation support. 3. Apply consistent TLS renegotiation configuration on all front- and backend proxies/load balancers. 4. Validate configuration by re-running a renegotiation test and a mitigation scan (for example using testssl.sh). ```conf
# Apache example (disable insecure renegotiation)
SSLInsecureRenegotiation Off

# Nginx/OpenSSL typically relies on the underlying stack; verify no insecure options are enabled and consult vendor docs for proxies
```
```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu example: upgrade TLS stack
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install --upgrade openssl libssl-dev
# Upgrade web server components as needed
sudo apt-get install --upgrade apache2 nginx
# Restart services after upgrades
sudo systemctl restart apache2
sudo systemctl restart nginx
```
```bash
# Re-test renegotiation support
./testssl.sh --renegotiation https://pentest-ground.com:4280
```
findings.remediation:
1. Apply vendor patches and upgrade to the latest TLS stack (OpenSSL and web server) that implement secure renegotiation per RFC 5746. 2. Disable insecure renegotiation across all devices (web servers, reverse proxies, load balancers) and enforce secure renegotiation in their TLS profiles. 3. Re-test end-to-end renegotiation status with testssl.sh and verify that renegotiation is secure and that insecure renegotiation is not supported. 4. Document the change, implement a change-control process, and monitor for TLS configuration drift. ```bash
# Example upgrade on a Debian/Ubuntu system
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade openssl libssl-dev
sudo apt-get upgrade apache2 nginx
sudo systemctl restart apache2 nginx
```
```conf
# Apache example (disable insecure renegotiation)
SSLInsecureRenegotiation Off
```
```bash
# Re-test after remediation
./testssl.sh --renegotiation https://pentest-ground.com:4280
# Optional: verify with SSL Labs
# https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/analyze.html?d=pentest-ground.com
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS secure renegotiation not supported; additional misconfigurations include missing security headers and cookie flags.
findings.exploitability: Requires network access in a MITM position; attacker can exploit insecure TLS renegotiation to influence the session without authentication.

findings.mitigation:
1. Harden edge/server TLS configuration to disable CBC and non-GCM ciphers, enforce TLS 1.2+ and prefer strong ephemeral ciphers. 
2. Validate changes against the live service and document baseline in the security policy. 
3. Limit negotiation to AEAD ciphers (GCM/ChaCha20-Poly1305) and ensure PFS via ECDHE. 
4. Schedule periodic revalidation on deployment changes and monitor cipher suites ongoing.

```nginx
# nginx TLS hardening (example)
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```

```apache
# Apache SSL config (example)
SSLProtocol -all +TLSv1.2 +TLSv1.3
SSLCipherSuite ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
SSLHonorCipherOrder on
SSLCompression Off
```

```bash
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:4280 -tls1_2 -servername pentest-ground.com
```

```bash
systemctl reload nginx  # or systemctl reload httpd
```
findings.remediation:
1. Implement a centralized TLS hardening standard and apply it across all frontends (load balancers, reverse proxies) and application servers. Include a policy to disable CBC/non-GCM ciphers and require TLS 1.2+/TLS 1.3. 

```nginx
# nginx TLS hardening (permanent)
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```

```apache
# Apache SSL config (permanent)
SSLProtocol -all +TLSv1.2 +TLSv1.3
SSLCipherSuite ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
SSLHonorCipherOrder on
SSLCompression Off
```

2. Permanently remove CBC/RSA-CBC and other weak suites from all servers. Ensure only AEAD ciphers (AES-GCM/ChaCha20) and ECDHE key exchange are enabled. 

3. Enable TLS 1.3 and keep TLS 1.2 as minimum, explicitly disabling TLS 1.0/1.1 across all platforms. 

```bash
# Validation after remediation
nmap --script ssl-enum-ciphers -p 4280 pentest-ground.com
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:4280 -tls1_2 -cipher 'ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384'
```

4. Integrate automated TLS posture checks into CI/CD and monthly baseline re-scans. Maintain a living configuration template and audit changelog after every patch or config update.
findings.llm_notes: TLS config exposes CBC-based ciphers for TLS 1.2; prefer AEAD ciphers (AES-GCM/ChaCha20-Poly1305) and enable security headers and cookie flags.
findings.exploitability: Attacker with network access can attempt to negotiate a CBC-based TLS 1.2 cipher; success depends on client support and downgrade protections, but data confidentiality could be weakened due to weaker cipher suites.

findings.title: Stack Trace Disclosure
findings.description:
Response discloses a PHP stack trace or error message: Stack trace:
#0
Path: /vulnerabilities/sqli/

raw.info:
Response discloses a PHP stack trace or error message: Stack trace:
#0
raw.http_request:
GET /vulnerabilities/sqli/?id=default&Submit=Submit HTTP/1.1
host: pentest-ground.com:4280
connection: keep-alive
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:128.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/128.0
accept-language: en-US
accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
cookie: security=low; PHPSESSID=e117461e290acd336e381f2223605186
raw._poc_evidence:
PoC poc-010 (python) — verdict: failed
Exit code: 1
--- stdout ---
HTTP status: 200
Body length: 4064
Indicator not found

--- stderr ---

findings.mitigation:
1. Disable display of errors in production and enable logging.
2. Enforce runtime non-disclosure of errors in code.
3. Ensure web server configuration does not leak error details.
4. Route all errors to generic user-friendly pages.

```ini
; php.ini (production)
display_errors = Off
log_errors = On
error_log = /var/log/php-errors.log
```

```php
// Production bootstrap
ini_set('display_errors', '0');
ini_set('log_errors', '1');
```

```apache
# Apache vhost
php_flag display_errors Off
```

```php
// Generic error response (do not reveal traces)
http_response_code(500);
echo 'Internal Server Error';
```
findings.llm_notes: PHP stack traces and full path disclosures indicate improper error handling and information leakage.
findings.exploitability: Attacker can trigger a server error by requesting a vulnerable URL to reveal a PHP stack trace and file paths; no authentication beyond HTTP(S) access is required.

raw._poc_evidence:
PoC poc-016 (bash) — verdict: failed
Exit code: 1
--- stdout ---

--- stderr ---

findings.mitigation:
1. Enforce HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite cookie attributes in the application. Use framework-specific settings to ensure cookies are not accessible via JavaScript and are transmitted only over HTTPS.
```js
# Node.js (Express)
app.use((req, res, next) => {
  res.cookie('session', req.sessionID, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'Lax' });
  next();
});
```
```py
# Django
SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True
SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY = True
CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True
CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY = True
CSRF_COOKIE_SAMESITE = 'Lax'
```

2. Enable HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) on the web server.
```nginx
# nginx
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
```
```apache
# Apache
Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload"
```

3. Add Content-Security-Policy (CSP) and X-Frame-Options to mitigate content-based attacks and clickjacking.
```nginx
# nginx
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https:; object-src 'none'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
```
```apache
# Apache
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https:; object-src 'none'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; frame-ancestors 'self'"
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
```

4. Harden TLS configuration (disable weak protocols/ciphers and enable modern TLS).
```nginx
# nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```
```apache
# Apache
SSLProtocol all -SSLv3 -TLSv1 -TLSv1.1
SSLCipherSuite HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5:!3DES
SSLHonorCipherOrder on
```
findings.remediation:
1. Obtain and install a valid CA-signed certificate (and serve the complete chain). If using Let’s Encrypt: 
```bash
certbot certonly --nginx -d pentest-ground.com -d www.pentest-ground.com
```
Then ensure the server points to the new chain:
```nginx
ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/pentest-ground.com/fullchain.pem;
ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/pentest-ground.com/privkey.pem;
```

2. Enforce modern TLS on the server (TLS 1.2/1.3) and disable legacy protocols/ciphers.
```nginx
# nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```

3. Implement security headers and ensure cookie attributes in code and server config.
```nginx
# nginx
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https:; object-src 'none'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
```
```py
# Django (cookie hygiene reminder in code)
SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE = True
SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY = True
CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE = True
CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY = True
CSRF_COOKIE_SAMESITE = 'Lax'
```

4. Verify and monitor post-remediation.
```bash
# TLS/headers verification
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:5013 -servername pentest-ground.com
curl -I https://pentest-ground.com:5013
# Optional: SSL Labs test (external): https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/
```
findings.llm_notes: Wapiti flags TLS misconfigs (no CSP, no HttpOnly/Secure cookies, no HSTS) plus a non-EV certificate and outdated OpenSSL CCS vulnerability reference; potential full path disclosure detected at /solutions: /usr/bin/env.
findings.exploitability: Remote attacker must intercept or impersonate TLS traffic (MITM) to exploit missing HttpOnly/Secure flags, CSP, X-Frame-Options, and HSTS; no direct code execution. Primary risk is information leakage and session hijacking.

raw._poc_evidence:
PoC poc-016 (bash) — verdict: failed
Exit code: 1
--- stdout ---

--- stderr ---

findings.mitigation:
immediate workaround — 4 steps:
1. Enable OCSP stapling on the TLS terminator. For Nginx:
```nginx
ssl_stapling on;
ssl_stapling_verify on;
resolver 8.8.8.8 1.1.1.1;
ssl_trusted_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt;
```
2. Ensure a complete certificate chain is presented (intermediates included). Example:
```bash
# Nginx/Apache-style references; adjust paths to your environment
ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/your_domain.crt;
ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/private/your_domain.key;
ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/ca_chain.pem;  # intermediates
```
3. Verify OCSP stapling during TLS handshake:
```bash
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:5013 -servername pentest-ground.com </dev/null 2>&1 | grep -i ocsp
```
4. If you require OCSP Must-Staple, reissue the certificate to include the TLS Feature (OCSP Must-Staple) extension and verify the presence of the extension:
```bash
openssl x509 -in /path/to/cert.pem -text -noout | grep -A2 TLS-Feature
```
findings.remediation:
permanent fix — 4 steps:
1. Reissue or obtain a certificate that includes OCSP Must-Staple (TLS Feature extension 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.1.24) from your CA, or request enabling Must-Staple on the certificate.
2. Install the new certificate and complete chain on the TLS terminator (web server/load balancer):
```bash
# Example for Nginx
ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/your_domain_must_staple.crt;
ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/private/your_domain.key;
ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/ca_chain.pem;
```
3. Ensure OCSP stapling remains enabled after reissue:
```nginx
ssl_stapling on;
ssl_stapling_verify on;
resolver 8.8.8.8 1.1.1.1;
```
4. Validate production configuration and certificate features:
```bash
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:5013 -servername pentest-ground.com -showcerts | openssl x509 -text -noout | grep -A2 TLS-Feature
# Optional external check
# SSL Labs/Equivalent: verify OCSP stapling = Yes and TLS Feature = OCSP Must-Staple
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS configuration lacks OCSP stapling and HSTS; certificate uses EC 128-bit key, expires in ~34 days; CVE-2014-0224 (OpenSSL CCS) noted.
findings.exploitability: Requires a MITM or traffic interception to influence the TLS handshake; no direct server access is required.

```

2. Ensure session cookies use Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite attributes to protect against cookie theft or hijacking.

```text
Set-Cookie: sessionId=REDACTED; Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict
```

3. Use a Certificate Transparency (CT) logged certificate and verify that SCTs are present. Reissue if necessary and ensure the certificate is logged in CT logs.

```bash
# Check for SCTs (if supported by OpenSSL/GUI tooling)
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:5013 -servername pentest-ground.com </dev/null 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -text | grep -i SCT -A 2
```

4. Harden TLS configuration by enabling TLS 1.2/1.3 and disabling legacy protocols/ciphers.

```nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256GCM-SHA384';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```
findings.remediation:
1. Acquire and install a CT-logged certificate for pentest-ground.com (replacing the current cert if needed) and reload the web server.

```bash
sudo cp /path/to/new/fullchain.pem /etc/ssl/certs/pentest-ground.com.pem
sudo cp /path/to/new/privkey.pem /etc/ssl/private/pentest-ground.com.key
sudo systemctl reload nginx  # or: sudo systemctl reload apache2
```

2. Enforce HSTS and modern TLS settings across the deployment.

```nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256GCM-SHA384';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
add_header Strict-Transport-Security 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload' always;
```

3. Update the application to set cookies with Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite attributes on all session cookies.

```text
# Example for a Node.js/Express app
res.cookie('sessionId', value, { secure: true, httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'Strict' });
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS misconfig and missing security headers observed; certificate uses 128-bit EC key and is near expiry; certificate transparency not enabled.
findings.exploitability: Remote attacker on the network can perform MITM or session hijacking due to missing HSTS, cookie flags, and TLS CT; no user interaction required.

findings.mitigation:
1. Disable insecure TLS renegotiation on affected servers to prevent MITM data injection. 2. Update OpenSSL and the web server to a version that supports secure renegotiation (RFC 5746) or TLS 1.3. 3. Enforce strong TLS configurations (TLS 1.2+/TLS 1.3, secure ciphers). 4. Validate the changes with a test tool and monitor for warnings. 

Examples:
```bash
# Apache (example)
SSLInsecureRenegotiation Off
systemctl reload httpd
```
```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu (OpenSSL upgrade)
apt-get update
apt-get install --only-upgrade openssl libssl1.1
systemctl restart apache2
```
```bash
# Apache TLS policy
SSLProtocol -all +TLSv1.2 +TLSv1.3
SSLCipherSuite HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5:!3DES
SSLHonorCipherOrder On
```
```bash
# Quick check
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:5013 -tls1_2
```
findings.remediation:
1. Apply vendor patches and upgrade TLS stack to latest releases that support secure renegotiation by default. 2. Reconfigure all affected services to disable insecure renegotiation and enable secure renegotiation with TLS 1.2+/TLS 1.3. 3. Prefer TLS 1.3 where possible and disable legacy protocols/ciphers. 4. Re-scan with testssl.sh or OpenSSL to confirm secure renegotiation is enforced and no fallback to insecure renegotiation remains. 

Commands you can run after patching:
```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu
apt-get update
apt-get upgrade --only-upgrade openssl libssl1.1
systemctl restart apache2
```
```bash
# Apache: enforce secure renegotiation and TLS policies (sample)
SSLInsecureRenegotiation Off
SSLProtocol -all +TLSv1.2 +TLSv1.3
SSLHonorCipherOrder On
```
```bash
# Validation
./testssl.sh --reneg pentest-ground.com:5013
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:5013 -tls1_2
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS renegotiation not secure; patch OpenSSL and enable secure renegotiation per RFC 5746; consider additional TLS hardening (HSTS, secure cookies).
findings.exploitability: Network attacker (MITM) can trigger TLS renegotiation and potentially inject data into the TLS session; requires being on-path to the victim's traffic.

```

```nginx
# Nginx (server block)
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
```

3. Validate changes with quick checks after reloads:

```bash
curl -I https://pentest-ground.com:9000/ | grep -i Strict-Transport-Security
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:9000 -tls1_2 </dev/null 2>/dev/null | grep -i renegotiation
```
findings.remediation:
1. Patch and upgrade the TLS stack to the latest OpenSSL/vendor release; restart services.

```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install --only-upgrade openssl libssl1.1
sudo systemctl restart apache2

# RHEL/CentOS
sudo yum update openssl
sudo systemctl restart httpd
```

2. Enforce TLS hardening on the server (disable insecure renegotiation by default; enable TLS 1.2/1.3 and modern ciphers).

```apache
# Apache (ssl.conf)
SSLInsecureRenegotiation off
SSLProtocol all -SSLv3 -TLSv1 -TLSv1.1
SSLCipherSuite HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5:!3DES
```

```nginx
# Nginx (server block)
server {
  listen 443 ssl;
  ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
  ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384';
  ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
}
```

3. Deploy HSTS for all HTTPS responses and plan a staged rollout (then consider preload after traffic shift).

```apache
Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload"
```

```nginx
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
```

4. Re-test with external/ internal scanners and monitor for insecure renegotiation attempts.

```bash
curl -I https://pentest-ground.com:9000/
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:9000 -tls1_2
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS insecure renegotiation detected along with OpenSSL CCS (CVE-2014-0224); HSTS not set; certificate uses EC-128 with 34 days remaining.
findings.exploitability: MITM access on the network path is required to trigger renegotiation; attacker could inject data during a TLS session if insecure renegotiation is accepted.

raw._poc_evidence:
PoC poc-020 (bash) — verdict: failed
Exit code: 1
--- stdout ---
HSTS header NOT detected on https://pentest-ground.com:9000/. This confirms the TLS/HSTS misconfiguration (no HSTS).

--- stderr ---

findings.mitigation: 1. Enable HSTS on the HTTPS endpoint immediately to prevent protocol downgrades. 2. Remove CBC/weak ciphers and enforce modern TLS, ensuring TLS 1.2/1.3 is used. 3. Apply changes to all front-end proxies/load balancers and origin servers, then restart services. 4. Validate quickly that HSTS header is present and that only strong ciphers are negotiated.
findings.remediation:
1. Harden TLS configuration across all endpoints (load balancers, reverse proxies, and web servers) to disable TLS 1.0/1.1 and CBC-based ciphers; prefer TLS 1.2+ and TLS 1.3 with ECDHE and AES-GCM.

```
# Nginx example
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```

```
# Apache example
SSLProtocol -all +TLSv1.2 +TLSv1.3
SSLCipherSuite HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5:!3DES:!AES128-SHA:!RC4
SSLHonorCipherOrder on
```

2. Enable HSTS with preload and apply to all subdomains; ensure responses set the header consistently.

```
# Nginx example
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;
```

```
# Apache example
Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload"
```

3. Deploy and verify changes across all tiers, then reload/restart services.

```
systemctl reload nginx
# or
systemctl reload httpd
```

4. Validate post-implementation using quick checks and a TLS scanner (e.g., testssl.sh or curl) to confirm HSTS presence and absence of weak ciphers.

```
curl -I https://pentest-ground.com:9000/ | grep -i Strict-Transport-Security
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS config accepts CBC-based ciphers for TLSv1.2 and lacks HSTS, with a certificate expiring in about 34 days.
findings.exploitability: Requires network access to the TLS endpoint; an attacker can initiate a TLS handshake using a CBC or weak cipher to observe potential downgrade or data exposure.

findings.mitigation:
1. Ensure server-side escaping for all user input used in HTML rendering.
2. Validate/sanitize the title at the input boundary with a whitelist (for example allow only letters, numbers, spaces, and .,_-).
3. Enable a Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header to restrict script execution and disallow inline scripts.
4. Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) rule to block reflected XSS in the title query parameter and enable logging/alerting.
findings.remediation:
1. Implement robust, consistent output encoding: treat title as data, escape before rendering in all templates; prefer framework auto-escaping.
2. Centralize input validation with a strict allowlist (regex: ^[A-Za-z0-9 .,_-]+$) and reject any non-conforming values at the API boundary.
3. Ensure application templates do not render raw user input; upgrade/patch templating engine to latest, enable auto-escaping by default.
4. Add/verify security controls (CSP, WAF) and re-test with automated scanners to confirm removal of XSS vector.
findings.llm_notes: Lack of CSP, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options; HttpOnly not set on SessionID cookie; cookies not marked Secure; HSTS not set; overall weak client-side protections increase risk of XSS and session hijacking.
findings.exploitability: Attacker crafts a URL to /2/edit?title=<payload> and lures a victim to click it; the payload executes in the victim's browser in the site's origin, enabling script execution and potential data leakage; no special prerequisites beyond user interaction.

3. Enforce a robust CSP and X-Content-Type-Options to reduce risk of inline/script execution.

findings.title: TLS/SSL misconfigurations
findings.description:
Certificate doesn't use Extended Validation
Path: /

raw.http_request:
GET / HTTP/1.1
host: pentest-ground.com:81
connection: keep-alive
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:128.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/128.0
accept-language: en-US
accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8

findings.remediation:
1. Deploy a production-grade TLS configuration with current libraries: disable old protocols, enable TLS 1.2/1.3, use strong ECDHE ciphers, and turn off session tickets.
```nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
ssl_session_tickets off;
```
2. Obtain and install a trusted certificate (prefer DV/EV per policy) and bind to port 443 on a valid hostname; ensure full certificate chain is served.
```bash
# Example with Certbot (NGINX)
sudo apt-get install certbot python3-certbot-nginx
sudo certbot --nginx -d pentest-ground.com -d www.pentest-ground.com
```
Verify chain and validity:
```bash
openssl x509 -in /etc/letsencrypt/live/pentest-ground.com/fullchain.pem -noout -dates
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:443 -servername pentest-ground.com
```
3. Secure cookies across the app: enforce HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite, and enable cookie-based CSRF protections as appropriate.
```js
// Node.js Express example
app.use(require('cookie-session')({ name: 'session', keys: ['KEY'], httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'Strict' }));
```
4. Validate after change: run post-deployment TLS/SSL checks and monitor for TLS renegotiation issues.
```bash
git clone https://github.com/drwetter/testssl.sh.git
./testssl.sh pentest-ground.com:443
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS/SSL misconfig includes non-EV cert with short expiry and missing security headers/cookie flags; potential exposure includes session tokens and lack of defense-in-depth.
findings.exploitability: Remote, no authentication required; attacker on the network can attempt MITM to capture/modify traffic due to TLS misconfig, and lack of HttpOnly/Secure cookies enables session theft via XSS; OpenSSL CCS vulnerability and lack of secure renegotiation add risk.

findings.title: TLS/SSL misconfigurations
findings.description:
OCSP Must-Staple extension is missing
Path: /

raw.http_request:
GET / HTTP/1.1
host: pentest-ground.com:81
connection: keep-alive
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:128.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/128.0
accept-language: en-US
accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8

findings.mitigation:
1. Enable OCSP stapling on the web server to return a stapled OCSP response during TLS handshakes.
2. Verify stapling is active and the OCSP responder is reachable; fix DNS/resolver or firewall issues if stapling fails.
3. If the certificate authority supports OCSP Must-Staple, ensure the certificate is issued with the TLS Feature extension status_request; otherwise use standard stapling.
findings.remediation:
1. Reissue the certificate with TLS Feature: status_request (OCSP Must-Staple) and deploy across all endpoints.
2. Confirm server configurations enable and enforce OCSP stapling and that the CA bundle/OCSP responders are reachable.
3. Validate with:

```bash
openssl s_client -connect pentest-ground.com:81 -servername pentest-ground.com -status
```
4. Monitor and maintain revocation tooling and stapling health; plan annual revocation/renewal reviews.
findings.llm_notes: TLS misconfig includes missing OCSP Must-Staple, no HSTS, insecure cookie flags and no CSP/X-Frame-Options; OpenSSL CCS vulnerability indicated but may depend on server/openssl version.
findings.exploitability: Requires network access and a MITM position to exploit OCSP revocation gaps; no user interaction needed.

findings.llm_notes: TLS misconfiguration and missing security headers/cookie flags elevate risk of credential/session theft.
findings.exploitability: Remote, no user interaction required. An attacker on the network path can attempt session hijacking or MITM due to insecure cookies (HttpOnly/Secure not set) and missing HSTS/certificate transparency.

findings.llm_notes: HSTS missing plus insecure cookies and legacy TLS issues indicate weak TLS posture; fix by enabling HSTS, setting HttpOnly and Secure on cookies, configuring TLS properly, and addressing OpenSSL CCS and renegotiation protections.
findings.exploitability: Requires a network attacker in a position to intercept or downgrade user traffic; with HSTS off, an attacker can attempt protocol downgrade to HTTP and, combined with missing cookie safeguards, potentially access session cookies.

findings.title: TLS/SSL misconfigurations
findings.description:
Server doesn't support secure renegotiations
Path: /

raw.http_request:
GET / HTTP/1.1
host: pentest-ground.com:81
connection: keep-alive
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:128.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/128.0
accept-language: en-US
accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8

findings.mitigation:
1. Patch OpenSSL and the web server to patched versions that disable insecure renegotiation (CVE-2014-0224). 2. Remove any insecure renegotiation directives (e.g., SSLInsecureRenegotiation) or ensure they are set to Off. 3. Restart affected services. 4. Validate secure renegotiation is enforced using quick checks.

```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu
apt-get update
apt-get install --only-upgrade openssl libssl-dev apache2
systemctl restart apache2

# CentOS/RHEL
yum update openssl
systemctl restart httpd
```
findings.remediation:
1. Establish a permanent patching process for OpenSSL and web servers to ensure timely security updates. 2. Harden TLS configuration by disabling legacy protocols (e.g., SSLv3, TLS <= 1.0) and enabling TLS 1.2/1.3 with strong ciphers. 3. Enforce secure renegotiation by ensuring SSLInsecureRenegotiation is Off or removed, and that servers reject insecure renegotiation attempts. 4. Re-test thoroughly with testssl.sh or SSL Labs to confirm the vulnerability is mitigated and secure renegotiation is active.

```bash
# nginx example (as part of hardening)
# 1) Update packages (example for Debian/Ubuntu)
apt-get update
apt-get install --only-upgrade openssl nginx
systemctl restart nginx

# 2) Harden TLS in nginx.conf
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:...';
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;

# Apache example (ssl.conf)
SSLProtocol all -SSLv3 -TLSv1 -TLSv1.1
SSLCipherSuite HIGH:!aNULL:!MD5:!3DES
SSLInsecureRenegotiation Off
systemctl restart httpd
```
findings.llm_notes: TLS misconfig: CCS vulnerability present, HSTS not set, cookies without Secure/HttpOnly, no CSP/X-Frame-Options; certificate uses EC 128-bit, not EV; port 81 in use.
findings.exploitability: Network-based; requires a MITM position to trigger the CCS ChangeCipherSpec vulnerability (CVE-2014-0224) to reveal/alter TLS traffic.

findings.title: TLS/SSL misconfigurations
findings.description:
The following ciphers are acceptable for TLSv1.2: TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256, TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA384
Path: /

raw.info: The following ciphers are acceptable for TLSv1.2: TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256, TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA384
raw.http_request:
GET / HTTP/1.1
host: pentest-ground.com:81
connection: keep-alive
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:128.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/128.0
accept-language: en-US
accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8

findings.mitigation:
1) Disable TLS 0.x/1.1 and CBC-mode ciphers; enforce TLS 1.2+/TLS 1.3 with AEAD ciphers. Use per-server configs:
```bash
# Apache (mod_ssl)
SSLProtocol -all +TLSv1.2 +TLSv1.3
SSLCipherSuite ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
SSLHonorCipherOrder On
```
```bash
# Nginx
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_ciphers ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256;
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;
```

2) Patch OpenSSL to latest and bounce services:
```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu
apt-get update
apt-get install --only-upgrade openssl
systemctl restart apache2  # or nginx
```

3) Re-test with a TLS scanner to verify only TLSv1.2/1.3 and AEAD ciphers are advertised:
```bash
nmap --script ssl-enum-ciphers -p 81 pentest-ground.com
```

4) Optionally enable TLS 1.3 and HSTS for additional hardening.
findings.remediation:
1) Deploy permanent TLS hardening: enable TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 across all front-ends; remove all CBC and legacy ciphers; ensure only AEAD suites are allowed. Use the same per-server blocks as above.

2) Update OpenSSL and recompile/redeploy server software (Apache/Nginx) to ensure no CVE-2014-0224 exposure; restart services and verify with tests.

3) Enforce TLS configuration in load balancers/reverse proxies and CI/CD pipelines; document change-management; enable TLS 1.3 where supported and enable HSTS.

4) Implement ongoing validation: schedule regular automated TLS scans and implement alerting for weak ciphers or deprecated protocols; verify at least quarterly.
findings.llm_notes: TLS misconfiguration includes vulnerable OpenSSL CCS and lack of security headers (HSTS, Secure/HttpOnly cookies) and missing TLS v1.3 support.
findings.exploitability: Requires a network-level MITM on the path to the server; if a vulnerable OpenSSL/ChangeCipherSpec handling is triggered by the handshake, TLS traffic could be decrypted/modified. Prerequisites: attacker can position themselves between client and server and the server is using a vulnerable OpenSSL version (CVE-2014-0224) and TLS handshake is exposed.

results.raw_output:
flag provided but not defined: -u
Usage of cariddi:
  -c int
    	Concurrency level. (default 20)
  -cache
    	Use the .cariddi_cache folder as cache.
  -d int
    	Delay between a page crawled and another.
  -debug
    	Print debug information while crawling.
  -e	Hunt for juicy endpoints.
  -ef string
    	Use an external file (txt, one per line) to use custom parameters for endpoints hunting.
  -err
    	Hunt for errors in websites.
  -examples
    	Print the examples.
  -ext int
    	Hunt for juicy file extensions. Integer from 1(juicy) to 7(not juicy).
  -h	Print the help.
  -headers string
    	Use custom headers for each request E.g. -headers "Cookie: auth=yes;;Client: type=2".
  -headersfile string
    	Read from an external file custom headers (same format of headers flag).
  -i string
    	Ignore the URL containing at least one of the elements of this array.
  -ie value
    	Comma-separated list of extensions to ignore while scanning
  -info
    	Hunt for useful informations in websites.
  -intensive
    	Crawl searching for resources matching 2nd level domain.
  -it string
    	Ignore the URL containing at least one of the lines of this file.
  -json
    	Print the output as JSON in stdout.
  -md int
    	Maximum depth level the crawler will follow from the initial target URL.
  -oh string
    	Write the output into an HTML file.
  -ot string
    	Write the output into a TXT file.
  -plain
    	Print only results.
  -proxy string
    	Set a Proxy, http and socks5 supported.
  -rua
    	Use a random browser user agent on every request.
  -s	Hunt for secrets.
  -sf string
    	Use an external file (txt, one per line) to use custom regexes for secrets hunting.
  -sr
    	Store HTTP responses.
  -t int
    	Set timeout for the requests. (default 10)
  -ua string
    	Use a custom User Agent.
  -version
    	Print the version.

executive_summary: Findings show widespread TLS/SSL misconfigurations across multiple endpoints, creating MITM risk and potential data exposure due to missing HSTS, certificate transparency gaps, weak cipher suites, and insecure renegotiation. CSP policy gaps and mirrored client-side vulnerabilities (reflected XSS) along with stack-trace disclosures expand the attack surface and enable content injection, session data leakage, and information exposure. Top priority actions are to harden TLS across all endpoints (enable HSTS, enforce certificate transparency, enable OCSP stapling, use strong ciphers, disable insecure renegotiation, secure cookies) and implement robust CSP policies (object-src, base-uri, frame-ancestors) while mitigating XSS vectors and production error disclosures.
